pages.raw_text:
Councilman Pratt last night
struck another blow for the initia-
tive and referendum ordinance
when he succeeded in having cor
poration counsel Geraghty instruct
ed to draw up an amendment to
the city charter providing for the
adoption of popular rule In
municipal government The motion
was passed without opposition and
it is possible that the people will
have an opportunity to vote for this
reform law at the coming election.


An advisory ordinance was intro
duced by Mr.. Pratt several months
ago, but it has been sidetracked in
one way or another. The last
heard of this ordinance 1t was in
the hands of Assistant Corporation
Counsel Winston, who says he will
report it some day. In view of the
friendly relations referendum adj
vocates believe exist between Mr..
Winston and some of the interests
that are opposed to the initiative
and referendum an early report is
not expected. If the move on the
part of Mr.. Pratt is successful it
will not matter what disposition is
made Of the advisory ordinance. as
the adoption of the law will be put
squarely up to the people as a
charter amendment, it is claimed.
